name: Cucumber Raita
tagline: Cooling yogurt-cucumber salad — probiotics, hydration, low calories

hero.alt: White raita in a bowl studded with green cucumber and dusted with roasted cumin powder
hero.caption: cucumber raita — cool, fresh, cumin-dusted

steps.title: Prep the cucumber
steps.detail: Grate the cucumber on a large hole grater. Squeeze out the excess water (drink that — it's gold) and keep the squeezed pulp.
steps.tip: Salt the cucumber for 5 min before squeezing to draw out even more water — keeps the raita from going watery.

steps.title: Whisk the yogurt
steps.detail: Whisk yogurt with a splash of water until smooth and pourable. Stir in roasted cumin powder and black salt.
steps.tip: If your yogurt is sour, add ½ tsp sugar to round it out.

steps.title: Combine and chill
steps.detail: Fold in the squeezed cucumber pulp, coriander, and mint. Chill 15 minutes before serving alongside biryani, paratha, or any spicy curry.
steps.tip: Add the cucumber just before serving if you want maximum crunch.

healthFacts: Live yogurt cultures + cucumber water = hydrating with electrolytes.
200g serving has ~100 calories, 6g protein, and 200mg calcium.
Cucumber adds vitamin K, potassium, and silica — gentle on stomach and kidneys.
Roasted cumin is anti-bloating; mint adds polyphenols and a cooling pop.
